# UC-17.1.37 · ISE-Cisco ACI / Cisco Secure Workload SGT Binding Synchronisation

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE labels users by group, but the data-centre fabric and the workload-protection tool need to know about the same groups. We make sure those tools agree with ISE — and ring the bell when one of them refuses a label, so security rules stay consistent.*

---

## Description

Detects SGT-to-EPG (Cisco ACI Endpoint Group) binding synchronisation failures and SGT-to-Secure-Workload-scope binding update failures — symptoms of integration drift between ISE TrustSec policy and Cisco ACI / Secure Workload enforcement.

## Value

When ISE's TrustSec policy and ACI/Secure Workload enforcement diverge, traffic that ISE classified as 'production' may be enforced by ACI as 'staging' (or vice versa). Catching binding-update failures in real time prevents silent classification drift.

## Implementation

Onboard ACI APIC syslog into `index=network`. Onboard Secure Workload binding events via the OpenAPI poller. Schedule the search every 30 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

On a lab ACI fabric, delete an EPG that ISE has an active SGT-to-EPG binding for. ISE emits 83022 (binding update failed). UC-17.1.37 fires showing the (sgt, epg) pair with `failures > 0`.

### Negative scenario

Recreate the EPG. ISE re-syncs and emits 83020 (binding created). UC-17.1.37 does not fire on the next schedule.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE-ACI integration configured (Administration > pxGrid Services > ACI Settings).
- ACI APIC syslog forwarded to Splunk.
- Splunk credentials for the Secure Workload OpenAPI; modular input or cron poller.
- `sgt_epg_mapping.csv` lookup (SGT → expected EPG).

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable ISE *TrustSec* category at INFO. Verify ACI APIC syslog is arriving at `index=network sourcetype=cisco:apic`.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 30 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke a binding failure as in controlTest. Confirm fire.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard panels listed above. Slack notification to `#network-segmentation-platform`.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **No ACI syslog events** — APIC syslog target not configured. Configure under APIC: Admin > External Data Collectors > Syslog.
- **Binding-failure events from ISE without ACI events** — APIC may be reachable but Secure Workload integration broken. Validate Secure Workload OpenAPI auth.
- **Persistent failures for one SGT** — That SGT has no matching EPG defined in ACI. Coordinate with the ACI admin to create or rename the EPG.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83020, 83021, 83022))
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:apic" event_type IN ("epg_static_path_change", "EPG-binding-failure"))
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="83022" OR event_type=="EPG-binding-failure")) as failures by sgt_name, epg_name
| where failures > 0
| sort - failures
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: count of failed bindings last 24h. (2) Sankey: sgt_name → epg_name → status (success/failure). (3) Top-N table: SGTs with most failed bindings.

## Known False Positives

- **Bulk ACI EPG renames** — A scheduled rename causes a transient binding miss until ACI re-resolves. Suppress within scheduled change windows.
- **Secure Workload scope reorganisation** — Major scope-tree changes generate binding update activity that may transiently fail before the new tree is applied.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — TrustSec / ACI Integration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
- [Cisco APIC — REST API](https://developer.cisco.com/docs/aci/)
